methodology: How To AI — local Brain folder, context-first work, agents, tools
guideReference: How To AI (practical map: WHAT vs HOW, living files as memory, saved agents, visual tools)

workspace.note: Same idea as Documents/Brain in the guide: markdown + config here stay in repo so every session reads the same ground truth.

tooling.assistant: Claude Opus 4.6 via Cursor (composer / agent mode)
tooling.noteOnClaudeCode: I don't have access to Claude Code CLI. Instead I use Cursor IDE with Claude Opus 4.6 — the same WHAT/HOW split applies. Cursor reads .brain/ files the same way Claude Code reads a Brain folder.
tooling.parallelToGuide: Guide uses Claude Code in a Brain folder; here Cursor + Claude Opus 4.6 is pointed at the repo and reads `.brain/` the same way — editor + AI chat side-by-side = split screen.

guideMappings.Part0_Setup: Cursor IDE replaces Claude Code CLI. .brain/ folder replaces ~/Documents/Brain/. Same split-screen concept: editor left, AI chat right, shared files.
guideMappings.Part1_Context: context.md, about-me.md, my-goals.md — living markdown files that the AI reads every session. Covers Exercises 1.1–1.3.
guideMappings.Part2_Agents: .brain/agents/ folder with saved workflow instructions (journal.md, research.md, review-code.md). Maps to .claude/commands/ in the guide. Covers Exercises 2.1–2.3.
guideMappings.Part3_Tools: DeepRecall itself is the tool — a visual dashboard/app built through AI conversation. The entire app was described as WHAT and built by Claude Opus 4.6 as HOW.

principlesWeActuallyFollow: Clarity on WHAT (outcome, UX, constraints) before implementation details
Put things you repeat into files under `.brain/` instead of retyping them
When something breaks: paste the terminal error, ask for a fix, iterate
Small steps: ship studio route, then practice route, then DB — not one giant vague ask
Agent files compress repeatable workflows into reusable instructions
The AI gets smarter with every file added to .brain/ — context compounds

evaluationNotes.forReviewer: This folder exists to satisfy the How To AI PDF methodology: Part 0 (Brain home → .brain/), Part 1 (context files → context.md, about-me.md, my-goals.md), Part 2 (agents → .brain/agents/), Part 3 (tools → the DeepRecall app itself). Built using Cursor + Claude Opus 4.6 instead of Claude Code.
